tidyfault 0.0.0.9
  • Reference

    tabulate() Function

    tabulate.Rd

    This function tabulates a data.frame of N minimum cutsets in your fault tree, describing each mincut set, a query used to filter a table of all sets, the number of cutsets that include that mincut, the total number of cutsets leading to failure, and the coverage (percentage of cutsets covered out of total failures) for that mincut.

    tabulate(
  data,
  formula,
  method = c("mocus_rcpp", "mocus_r", "mocus_original"),
  query = FALSE
)

    Arguments

    data

    (Required) Output from concentrate() function. Expects a character vector of minimum cutset expressions (e.g., "A * B").

    formula

    (Required) Function output from formulate(). Used to generate the truth table for calculating coverage statistics.

    method

    (Optional) Character string specifying the method used in concentrate(). Default is "mocus_rcpp". Supported values are "mocus_rcpp", "mocus_r", and "mocus_original".

    query

    (Optional) Logical - Whether to include the query column in the output. Default is FALSE.

    Value

    A data.frame (tibble) with one row per minimum cutset, containing:

    • mincut: Character string representing the minimum cutset as a boolean expression (e.g., "A * B" for events A AND B)

    • query: Character string containing a filter expression that can be used to identify truth table rows matching this cutset (e.g., "filter(A == 1, B == 1, outcome == 1)") (only included if query = TRUE)

    • cutsets: Integer count of truth table rows (cutsets) that match this minimum cutset and result in system failure

    • failures: Integer count of total truth table rows that result in system failure (same for all rows)

    • coverage: Numeric value (0 to 1) representing the proportion of failure cases covered by this minimum cutset. Calculated as cutsets / failures.

    Details

    This function analyzes minimum cutsets to quantify their importance in system failure:

    • Truth Table Generation: Uses formula to generate a complete truth table via calculate().

    • Cutset Parsing: Splits each minimum cutset expression into individual events and determines their required states (1 for occurrence, 0 for non-occurrence)

    • Query Construction: Creates filter expressions that identify truth table rows matching each cutset's event combination

    • Coverage Calculation: Counts how many failure cases (rows with outcome == 1) are covered by each minimum cutset, then calculates coverage as a proportion

    See also

    concentrate for generating minimum cutsets, formulate for creating the function used in truth table generation, calculate for generating truth tables

    Examples

    # Load dependencies
library(tidyverse)
library(tidyfault)

# Load example data into our environment
data("fakenodes")
data("fakeedges")

formula <- curate(nodes = fakenodes, edges = fakeedges) %>%
   equate() %>%
   formulate()
curate(nodes = fakenodes, edges = fakeedges) %>%
   concentrate(method = "mocus_rcpp") %>%
   tabulate(formula = formula, method = "mocus_rcpp")
#> # A tibble: 2 × 4
#>   mincut cutsets failures coverage
#>   <chr>    <int>    <int>    <dbl>
#> 1 A*B*D        2        5      0.4
#> 2 B*C          4        5      0.8
